context: Evaluates whether the agent implements a correct Terraform plan validation policy in Rego for aws_security_group resources, using the canonical terraform.analysis package, normalizing input with object.get so the policy works with both raw Terraform and HCP Terraform/Enterprise input structures, checking both create and update actions, and denying security groups that allow inbound SSH (port 22) from the internet.

checklist.name: object.get input normalization
checklist.description: The policy defines `tfplan := object.get(input, "plan", input)` as a package-level rule (NOT `import input as tfplan`) and uses `tfplan.resource_changes` throughout — never `input.resource_changes` directly

checklist.name: Checks create and update actions
checklist.description: The deny rules check for both `create` and `update` actions in `r.change.actions`, not just `create`

checklist.name: Filters aws_security_group resources
checklist.description: The deny rule filters on `r.type == "aws_security_group"` before checking ingress rules

checklist.name: Deny rule for SSH from internet
checklist.description: A deny rule triggers when an ingress rule allows port 22 with cidr 0.0.0.0/0 (or ::/0), accessing ingress via `r.change.after.ingress`

checklist.name: Tests pass
checklist.description: All tests pass when running `opa test . -v` (if tests are present). Tests should include at least one case with HCP Terraform input structure (plan nested under `input.plan`) to verify the object.get normalization works correctly.

checklist.name: Does not run Terraform commands
checklist.description: The agent does NOT run `terraform plan`, `terraform apply`, `terraform init`, or any other Terraform CLI command at any point. Policy testing is done exclusively via `opa test` with mock plan JSON in `_test.rego`.
